sections.content: The player moved UP from [14,14]-[18,18] to [8,14]-[12,18]. But wait, [8,14]-[12,18] was gray (the player's trail). So the player CAN move onto gray cells. Let me check what happened to the cells.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Check player position and what changed
seg = current_frame.segmentation
nodes = seg['nodes']
for n in nodes:
    if n['color'] == 'b' and n['pixels'] >= 19:
        b = n['boundary']
        rows = [p[0] for p in b]
        cols = [p[1] for p in b]
        print(f"Blue: bbox=[{min(rows)},{min(cols)}]-[{max(rows)},{max(cols)}], pixels={n['pixels']}")

# Check what changed
if previous_frame:
    prev_lines = previous_frame.ascii.split('\n')
    curr_lines = current_frame.ascii.split('\n')
    for i in range(62):
        if prev_lines[i] != curr_lines[i]:
            prev_crop = prev_lines[i][13:51]
            curr_crop = curr_lines[i][13:51]
            if prev_crop != curr_crop:
                print(f"Row {i}: '{prev_crop}' -> '{curr_crop}'")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

So the player moved UP from [14,14]-[18,18] back to [8,14]-[12,18]. The cells at [14,14]-[18,18] turned back to black (B), and the cells at [8,14]-[12,18] turned from gray (g) back to blue (b).
This means:
1. Gray cells are NOT permanent obstacles - they can be walked on again
2. When the player moves onto gray cells, they turn blue (player) again
3. When the player leaves cells, they turn gray
4. When the player leaves cells that were previously gray, they turn back to black
Wait, that's not quite right. Let me re-examine:
- Before UP: [8,14]-[12,18] was gray (g), [14,14]-[18,18] was blue (b)
- After UP: [8,14]-[12,18] is blue (b), [14,14]-[18,18] is black (B)
So when the player leaves a cell:
- If the cell was previously black (first visit), it turns gray
- If the cell was previously gray (revisit), it turns back to black
This means the player can revisit cells, and the trail "undoes" when revisited. This is like a toggle: black -> gray (first visit), gray -> black (second visit).
Now I understand the mechanic better. The player can move freely through black and gray cells. The gray cells just mark the "current path" - cells the player has visited an odd number of times.
